title: erophila vulgaris var. hirtella
intro: Erophila vulgaris var. hirtella, also known as Creeping Whitlow Grass, is a perennial herb belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its small, white or yellowish flowers and hairy stems. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Erophila vulgaris var. hirtella include its ability to soothe irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and act as an expectorant to relieve coughs and congestion. In herbal preparations, Erophila vulgaris var. hirtella is often used in infusions, tinctures, and ointments to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
